# NixOS System Configurations

This repository contains reproducible NixOS system configurations managed using **Nix Flakes** and **SOPS-Nix** for secrets.

## Repository Layout
* `flake.nix`: Entry point for system definitions.
* `configuration.nix`: Main system configuration for the laptop.
* `hardware-configuration.nix`: Autogenerated hardware configuration scan.
* `secrets.yaml`: Encrypted secrets (user password, etc.).
* `m913_config.ini`: Redragon M913 mouse profile configuration.
* `docs/`: Guides and system planning.
  * `docs/sops_setup.md`: Guide to setting up encrypted secrets.
  * `docs/dendritic_plan.md`: Roadmap for future modularization.

## How to Rebuild
To apply configuration changes on the local machine:
```bash
sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#laptop
```

## Secrets Management
Secrets are encrypted using SOPS and AGE. They are configured in `secrets.yaml` and loaded dynamically via `sops-nix` modules. See [sops_setup.md](docs/sops_setup.md) for keys management.

## One-time Mouse Configuration (Redragon M913)
The Redragon M913 mouse profile is configured via `m913_config.ini`. To apply this configuration to the hardware's onboard memory, run the following command from the root of this repository:

```bash
sudo nix run github:ZebWilson/m913-ctl-flake -- m913-ctl --config ./m913_config.ini
```
*Note: This writes the configuration directly to the mouse hardware memory. It only needs to be run once or whenever you modify the button mappings in `m913_config.ini`.*
